items.briefing:
Australian authorities have issued a warning about a global campaign targeting websites running vulnerable content management systems (CMS) like WordPress and similar platforms — this is directly relevant to any training organisation running a public website. Separately, a new Android malware called RedHook can gain deep access to infected devices using a wireless debugging feature, posing a risk to staff using Android phones for work.

If your organisation uses a website CMS, ensure it and all its plugins are updated immediately. **Practical tip:** Ask your IT support or web provider to confirm your website software is fully patched and up to date.

featured.title: Australia warns of global campaign targeting vulnerable CMS platforms - BleepingComputer
featured.quote: Australian authorities warn of cyberattacks targeting vulnerable website platforms globally.
